lesson: When the same HTR FAIL repeats night after night and continuity shows the mix sensor going open-circuit once hot, the sensor is the culprit. Re-running just burns another night.
symptoms: Display says HTR FAIL 12 and nothing else.
Manager says this is the third morning in a row.

tests.error-log: Heat-treat aborted three nights running — mix temp never registered above 40 °C even with the heater on.
tests.temp-probe: Independent probe says the mix hits temperature fine. The machine's own sensor disagrees.
tests.continuity-test: Mix temp sensor goes open-circuit above 40 °C — it fails exactly when the cycle needs it most.

flavour: The heat was real. The sensor was in denial.
authenticityNote: Sometimes the heat-treat lockout is genuine: a mix temp sensor that drops out when hot aborts every cycle. Re-running just burns another night — which is the trap, because re-running is the right call for the other HTR FAIL. The continuity test is what tells these twins apart.
